data.title: Bellatora announces corporate name change to Kinetic Seas and ticker symbol change to "KSEZ"
data.description:
• Kinetic Seas has officially changed its corporate name to Kinetic Seas (OTCPK:KSEZ).
  • This name change reflects the Co.'s expanded focus on delivering cutting-edge artificial intelligence solutions for businesses.
  • The Co.'s common stock, quoted on the OTC Pink marketplace under the former ticker symbol "ECGR" will continue to be quoted on the OTC Pink marketplace under the new ticker symbol KSEZ effective immediately.
  • "Our new name encapsulates the constant evolution of AI technology and our role in navigating businesses through this exciting, ever-changing landscape." said Edward Honour, CEO.

data.title: Atlassian keeps Buy rating at Canaccord
data.description:
Canaccord maintained its Buy rating on Atlassian (NASDAQ:TEAM) and raised the price target raised on the shares to $225 from $200.

The Australian company develops several software products, including a project management system called Jira, which connects technical and business teams.

The analysts said that their conversations with investors have been upbeat — the scope of the opportunity, the commitment to margin re-expansion, and the 'new' guidance philosophy all stand out. In relation to guidance, the fiscal first quarter results will be a good test with respect to just how conservatively recast Cloud growth estimates are for the fiscal year 2025.

The analysts think there could be a few points of upside to Atlassian's fiscal first quarter guidance of 27% Cloud growth, which they think could serve as an important first step in unlocking a gradual re-rating of the company's stock higher.

Atlassian (TEAM) has a Hold rating at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system, which consistently beats the market. Meanwhile, the Seeking Alpha authors' average rating is more positive with a Buy, and so is the average Wall Street analysts' rating, Buy.

data.title: bioAffinity Technologies names J. Michael Edwards as CFO
data.description:
• bioAffinity Technologies (BIAFW) Thursday said that J. Michael Edwards has accepted the position of Chief Financial Officer.
  • Edwards has been bioAffinity’s interim CFO since Sept. 15 and previously served as bioAffinity’s consulting CFO from 2014 to 2023, overseeing the company’s initial public offering in 2022.
  • Edwards will report directly to bioAffinity Technologies (BIAFW) President and CEO Maria Zannes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: United Airlines sets itself apart with aggressive international expansion plans
data.description:
United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) announced on Thursday the largest international expansion in its history, with service to eight new cities.

Starting in May 2025, the carrier will launch five new nonstop flights from its hub in Newark/New York to destinations no other U.S. airline. The new destinations include Nuuk, Greenland; Palermo, Italy; Bilbao, Spain; Madeira Island, Portugal and Faro, Portugal. The airline is also adding three new nonstop routes from Washington D.C./Dulles, including its first-ever flight to Dakar, Senegal operating year-round and new seasonal nonstop flights to Nice, France and Venice, Italy.

Notably, United Airlines (UAL) is already the U.S. carrier with the most flights across the Atlantic. Looking ahead, the summer 2025 transatlantic schedule will be the largest in the company's history, with more than 760 weekly flights. In addition to new flights, customers will have even more time to explore Venice, Italy; Athens, Greece; and Barcelona, Spain with earlier start dates next summer. The airline will also increase the number of weekly direct flights between Newark/New York-Palma de Mallorca, Spain, Newark/New York-Dubrovnik, Croatia, and Newark/New York-Athens, Greece. United (UAL) is also adding new direct flights from Tokyo-Narita to Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia and Kaohsiung – destinations no other U.S. airline serves – and a new nonstop flight to Koror, Palau. Those new flights will connect in Tokyo to United's (UAL) transpacific services to five hub locations in the continental United States. In total, next summer, United will offer 800 daily flights to and from 147 international destinations – including 40 that no other U.S. airline serves.

Shares of United Airlines (UAL) fell 2.40% in premarket action following soft guidance from rival Delta Air Lines (DAL).

data.title: Jacobs secures CG Semi contract for engineering design of new OSAT Facility in India
data.description:
• Jacobs (NYSE:J) has been appointed by CG Semi Private for engineering design of its new outsourced semiconductor assembly and test facility at Sanand, Gujrat in India.
  • The main facility is planned to be built on more than 28 acres.
  • The OSAT facility will manufacture a variety of advanced and legacy packages for industries such as automotive, consumer, industrial and 5G.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PAVmed subsidiary, Veris Health, awarded $1.8M NIH grant to enhance cancer care
data.description:
• PAVmed (NASDAQ:PAVM) and its majority-owned digital health subsidiary, Veris Health, has been awarded a $1.8M grant from the National Institute on Minority Health and Health Disparities, an institute of the National Institutes of Health.
  • The two-year grant will fund research to optimize and validate the Veris Cancer Care Platform for the needs of medically underserved cancer patients, in partnership with an academic cancer center.
  • "This grant will accelerate our efforts to expand the reach and impact of our technology, while strengthening our relationships with academic cancer centers and within the oncology community."
  • The Co. look forward to bringing the benefits of this technology to broader and more diverse patient populations, to improve patient outcomes, reduce healthcare costs, and increase patient access to enhanced cancer care.

data.title: Zoom's price target raised at Benchmark following 'Zoomtopia' event
data.description:
Benchmark raised Zoom Video Communications (NASDAQ:ZM) price target to $85 from $83 after the company revised its long-term sustainable margin assessments.

The company unveiled the margin assessments on Wednesday at its Zoomtopia financial session.

The analysts said that CEO Eric Yuan's keynote and the range of new AI related products within Zoom's "federated" AI approach conveyed Zoom's superior pace of innovation off its AI-first platform and cloud emphasis compared to rivals, which are still held up by legacy approaches.

The company's announcements included AI Companion 2.0, Customer AI Companion paid add on, Zoom Workplace for Frontline Workers, Healthcare (with AI add on), and Education (with AI add on) and AI-first innovations for customer experience, marketing and sales.

Zoom also welcomed new CFO Michelle Chang, former Microsoft vice president and CFO of Modern Workplace (namely Office and Teams) & Cyber Security, replacing Kelly Steckelberg, the analysts added.

data.title: Connexa Sports shares jump after $30M licensing deal
data.description:
• Connexa Sports Technologies (NASDAQ:YYAI) the 20% owner of Yuanyu Enterprise Management, Thursday said that it has entered into an exclusive license agreement with Guofu Enterprise Management, a Hong Kong-based entity, covering Hong Kong, Japan, South Korea, and Southeast Asia.
  • Building on an earlier term sheet, this agreement calls for minimum royalty payments to YYEM of more than $30 million through 2026.
  • The agreement is expected to generate in excess of $30 million in license income for YYEM through the end of December 2026.
  • Connexa Sports Technologies (YYAI) rose +6.53% premarket to $8.48.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Roche collaboration for lead asset boosts Tempest
data.description:
• Shares of Tempest Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TPST) surged ~29% in the pre-market on Thursday after the company announced an agreement with Swiss pharma giant Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) to further develop its lead asset amezalpat (TPST-1120).
  • According to the deal, Roche (OTCQX:RHHBF) will supply its anti-PD-L1 immunotherapy atezolizumab (Tecentriq) to support a Phase 3 trial Tempest (TPST) expects to start in Q1 2025 targeting patients with hepatocellular carcinoma, a form of liver cancer.
  • The pivotal trial is designed to evaluate amezalpat in combination with the current standard of care, atezolizumab plus bevacizumab, as a first-line option for unresectable or metastatic hepatocellular carcinoma.
  • “Based on the positive Phase 2 data, I believe this combination therapy holds the potential to significantly improve first-line liver cancer treatment, and we look forward to amezalpat moving into this pivotal Phase 3 study,” TPST CEO Stephen Brady remarked.

data.title: Vireo Growth names Amber Shimpa as CEO
data.description:
• Vireo Growth (OTCQX:VREOF) said it has appointed Amber Shimpa to the role of CEO, effective immediately.
  • The company added Josh Rosen has stepped down as the CEO, interim CFO, and also from the company's board.
  • Joe Duxbury, who has been with the company since 2019, has been named as the interim CFO.
